title: Helm Chart Development
learningObjectives: Understand Helm chart structure and templating
Create and package custom Helm charts

theory.content:
What is Helm?

Helm is the package manager for Kubernetes. A chart is a collection of files describing a set of Kubernetes resources. Releases are instances of a chart deployed to a cluster.

Chart Structure

mychart/
  Chart.yaml          # Chart metadata
  values.yaml         # Default values
  templates/
    deployment.yaml   # Kubernetes manifests (Helm templates)
    service.yaml
    _helpers.tpl      # Named templates (partials)
  charts/             # Chart dependencies
  .helmignore

Chart.yaml

apiVersion: v2
name: mychart
description: A sample Helm chart
type: application
version: 0.1.0       # Chart version
appVersion: "1.0.0"  # App version

Templating

Helm templates use Go templating with the Sprig function library:

# deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: {{ include "mychart.fullname" . }}
  labels:
    {{- include "mychart.labels" . | nindent 4 }}
spec:
  replicas: {{ .Values.replicaCount }}
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: {{ .Chart.Name }}
          image: "{{ .Values.image.repository }}:{{ .Values.image.tag }}"
          ports:
            - containerPort: {{ .Values.service.port }}

values.yaml

replicaCount: 2
image:
  repository: nginx
  tag: "1.25"
service:
  type: ClusterIP
  port: 80

Key Commands

# Install
helm install myrelease ./mychart

# Override values
helm install myrelease ./mychart --set replicaCount=3
helm install myrelease ./mychart -f custom-values.yaml

# Upgrade
helm upgrade myrelease ./mychart --set image.tag=1.26

# Rollback
helm rollback myrelease 1

# List releases
helm list

# Debug/dry-run
helm install --dry-run --debug myrelease ./mychart

Chart Dependencies

# Chart.yaml
dependencies:
  - name: postgresql
    version: "12.x.x"
    repository: "https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami"

# Then run:
helm dependency update

Official Helm Charts Documentation →
